You can still score big savings on a new Apple Watch Series 9 model, but the best option comes from Amazon, where you will get a new model for as low as $330. Best Buy will let you pick one up for as low as $359, but you can make the price drop with an eligible trade-in.

$330 at Amazon $359 at Best Buy $399 at Apple
Apple Watch Ultra 2
$730 $799 Save $69

The Apple Watch Ultra 2 is the way to go if you want a new rugged Apple Watch, as its predecessor seems to be sold out almost everywhere. You can get yours for $730 with an on-page coupon, but you can also get one for less if you're willing to trade in one of your current devices to get up to $235 off at Best Buy.

$730 at Amazon $799 at Best Buy $799 at Apple
Apple Watch Series 8 Graphite
$320 $399 Save $79

Best Buy and Adorama are some of the last places you will find the Apple Watch Series 8 in new condition, and options start with the GPS-only model for $320. However, you can also consider picking up the 45mm LTE version with a Stainless Steel Case for $650 with $49 in instant savings.

$320 at Best Buy (GPS) $650 at Adorama LTE $399 at Adorama GPS
Apple Watch Series 7
$463 $749 Save $286

The Apple Wach Series 7 is still a beautiful alternative for those interested in a new Apple Watch with a Stainless Steel case that won't break the bank. It is now available for just $463 after a 38 percent discount. Indeed, it's not one of the latest versions around, but it will still deliver accurate fitness tracking and other excellent features for less.

$463 at Amazon
Midnight Apple Watch SE 2
$199 $249 Save $50

The most affordable Apple Watch model on the market has just become even more compelling, as you can now take the second generation Apple Watch SE with you for just $199 if you act fast, as Best Buy's current offers will get you a $50 instant discount on one. You can make it even more affordable by trading in one of your devices to get up to $90 extra savings.

$199 at Best Buy (GPS)

FAQ

Q: When can you get the best savings on a new Apple Watch?

Indeed, the best moment to pick up a new Apple Watch is just before the release of new models, but we already missed that window, as Apple released the Series 9 and the Ultra 2 back in September. You will also score great savings during special sales events, including Black Friday, Cyber Monday, and Amazon Prime Day, but those are also gone. Luckily for you, the last great chance to get massive savings on a new device comes with the holidays, and some of those great savings are already available, so don’t miss out. Check out the latest deals available on these outstanding products.

Q: Where can you get the best savings?

Amazon is probably the best place to score hot deals on Apple products, as these ordinarily last longer. You will also find great savings at Best Buy, and other retailers. And don’t worry if you miss out on a specific deal, as they tend to reappear after a couple of weeks.

Q: How can you tell if it’s a good deal?

The Apple Watch deals usually get you $49 to $50 in instant savings on the latest models, so anything above that makes it a killer deal. And if you want better savings, you can also consider going for previous models, which may give you more than $100 in savings if you’re lucky. And don't forget to check out the best cases to keep your investment safe from scratches and other accidents.

item.title: 6 simplest mods for mechanical keyboards
item.itunes:title: 6 simplest mods for mechanical keyboards
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Key Takeaways

  • Customize your keyboard with aftermarket keycaps and cables for a big makeover without spending a lot.
  • Tweak the feel and sound of your keyboard by trying out different switches.
  • Lubing your switches & stabilizers, and adding foam dampeners can make a massive difference to your typing experience.

There are endless reasons to love mechanical keyboards — satisfying typing and sound, great variety, and easy repairability. But, perhaps, the most fun part of mechanical keyboards is giving them your own touch by customizing and modifying them. Now, keyboard mods can range from the simplest ones that are quick and don't cost a lot to complicated and time-consuming ones that are kind of pricey.

This list will dabble into the easiest keyboard mods that anyone can do. All you need is some time and a few bucks to go from a keyboard noob to an enthusiast. The best mechanical keyboards can cost a lot but with these mods, even your budget keyboard can feel and sound like a custom-built mechanical keyboard.

6 Keycaps

Start from the outside in

Despite the impressive variety in form factors, features, and switches when buying mechanical keyboards, you'll rarely find the same in keycaps. You can choose from OEM, XDA, and Cherry profiles keycaps by shopping around for different keyboards, but in terms of looks, there are not many choices except white, black, and a few other colors (unless you consider expensive artisanal boards). This is why I recommend switching to some aftermarket keycaps to give your keyboard a big makeover.

For $15-$25, you can score some of the best keyboard keycaps from Amazon.

You don't need to spend around $50 for a set of keycaps from sites like Drop or Glorious. For $15-$25, you can score some of the best keyboard keycaps from Amazon. Whether you fancy some gradient keycaps with side-printed letters, pudding-style keycaps with RGB, or Matcha tea keycaps for earthy vibes, there are endless options you can browse for hours.

XVX Low Profile Keycaps
$21 $24 Save $3

For fans of MacBook and chiclet keyboards, the XVX low-profile keycaps are perfect. They're double-shot PBT and allow your backlight to shine through. And the best part? They cost just over $20.

$21 at Amazon

5 Cables

Wired keyboards can be fun, too

Source: Unsplash

Adding custom cables is a relatively newer keyboard mod. Thanks to a wide range of beautiful and affordable cables on the market, it's hard to resist switching your boring cable for an aftermarket one. You might even sacrifice wireless functionality in favor of a gorgeous coiled cable that enhances the looks of both your keyboard and your desk setup.

Some of the best wireless keyboards come with a detachable cable.

Adding a custom cable to your keyboard is super simple — you just need your keyboard to have a detachable cable. For those without one, you can still add a custom cable with some soldering, but that hardly counts as simple. Some of the best wireless keyboards come with a detachable cable for when you need wired usage, so you can have the best of both worlds.

Asceny Coiled Mechanical Keyboard Cable

Asceny's coiled and braided cables are some of the most affordable custom cables you can buy. There are around 7 color variants to choose from. The added durability and satisfying texture due to the PET double sleeve is a bonus.

$15 at Amazon

4 Switches

You've got a lot of "switching" options

Outside of mods like lubing and adding dampening materials, switches provide some of the easiest ways to modify the feel and sound of your keyboard. Check if you currently have blue or red stock switches that came with your keyboard. If you'd like to try switches that are less "clicky" sounding than your blue ones, you can switch to brown switches. They're less noisy than blue switches but still provide satisfying tactile feedback.

Switch preferences can vary drastically from person to person.

Similarly, you can switch from brown switches to red, yellow, or one of many other "linear" switches if you don't care for tactility but want a faster typing experience. Again, these are all my experiences, and you'd need to try out a few switches before reaching your own conclusions, as switch preferences can vary drastically from person to person.

The best gaming keyboard switches can be a good starting point when you're new to experimenting with mechanical switches.

Razer Yellow Linear Mechanical Keyboard Switches

These linear switches from Razer are affordable, durable, and come with sound dampeners for that silent yet satisfying sound. The 45gf actuation force is ideal for gamers and speed typers alike.

$25 at Amazon

3 Stabilizers

Add some stability to your life

Your mechanical keyboard might already have stabilizers for the largest keys like the spacebar, Shift, and Enter. These metallic supports reduce the unwanted movement of the keys and the rattling sound when typing. If you remove your keycaps and find no stabilizers, you can add some yourself to result in a more stable and deeper-sounding keypress.

A good set of stabilizers can turn your large keys from a noisy mess to a "thocky" delight.

While stabilizers might feel like an advanced mod due to the application and cost involved, they are one of the most impactful mechanical keyboard modifications you can make. A good set of stabilizers can turn your large keys from a noisy mess to a "thocky" delight.

DUROCK V1 Screw-in Stabilizers

The DUROCK V1 stabilizers work with most mechanical keyboards. They screw into your keyboard's PCB and come pre-clipped to save you some time.

$17 at Amazon

2 Lubing

It always helps!

Lubing your stabilizers and switches can have a huge impact on the sound and feel of your keyboard. By reducing the friction between the individual switch parts, you end up with smoother, less scratchy keystrokes. Similarly, lubed stabilizers help reduce the rattling noise that can often ruin your typing experience.

You'll appreciate the heightened quality of your typing experience

Lubing might look like a complicated process, but is actually quite simple. You just need to be patient with the process and not overdo the grease. Once you complete lubricating all of your switches and stabilizers, you'll appreciate the heightened quality of your typing experience.

Byhoo Keyboard Lube Kit

The Byhoo Lube Kit is a helpful all-in-one solution for lubing your mechanical keyboard switches and stabilizers.

$14 at Amazon

1 Foam dampeners

Simple yet effective

If you're adding stabilizers and lubricants to improve the sound of your keyboard, you should also try adding a dampening material to your PCB for a more comprehensive solution. Packing foam is one of the simplest ways to do this as it's often lying around the house or easily available as $10 rolls from Amazon.

You can experiment with other materials like kinetic sand, Play-Doh, cork boards, and even garden sand.

You just need to cut one to size by measuring it against your keyboard's PCB and then firmly insert it inside. You'll almost always get a deeper, fuller-sounding keyboard with a foam mod. In fact, you can experiment with other materials like kinetic sand, Play-Doh, cork boards, and even garden sand. But packing foam is probably the simplest to start with, as it's easily removable if you don't like the result.

Keyboard modding: A wholesome community

The world of keyboard modding might look intimidating from the outside but when you've got such a welcoming and helpful community, it's so much fun to pick apart and tweak your mechanical keyboard. Customization is one of the biggest reasons to buy a mechanical keyboard, and if you haven't tried it out yet, you're missing out.

You can learn about switches, keycaps, form factors, and more, and find out reasons to build your own mechanical keyboard from scratch. Check out the best budget wireless keyboards for some awesome options to get you started with a new and reading hobby.

item.title: Best Samsung Galaxy S23 cases in 2024
item.itunes:title: Best Samsung Galaxy S23 cases in 2024
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Although Samsung just released the new Galaxy S24 series, there are still plenty of reasons you still might want to pick up a Galaxy S23. For one, it's going to get all the new Galaxy AI features on the newer phones in the next few months. Samsung's Galaxy S23 comes with Corning's Gorilla Glass Victus 2, which is the same as the newer Galaxy S24. As a result, the phone should hold up great, like many of the best phones released this year.

However, that doesn't mean it's entirely immune to scratches and cracks, so we recommend ordering a protective case if you've just picked up a new Galaxy S23. Adding a case is also a nice way to make your older phone feel new and fresh now that it's going on a year old, if you bought in early. To help you find a great Galaxy S23 case that fits your needs, we've rounded up the best Galaxy S23 cases you can buy right now.

  • Spigen Ultra Hybrid S for Galaxy S23
    Editor's choice
    $20 at Amazon
  • dbrand Grip for Galaxy S23
    Premium pick
    $50 at dbrand
  • Supcase UB Pro for Galaxy S23
    Promoted pick
    $27 at Amazon
  • JETech Clear Case for Galaxy S23
    Best value
    $10 at Amazon
  • Otterbox Commuter Series Case for Galaxy S23
    Rugged case
    $19 at Amazon
  • Mous Limitless 5.0 for Galaxy S23
    Carbon fiber case
    $60 at Amazon
  • Spigen Tough Armor Case for Galaxy S23
    With kickstand
    $21 at Amazon
  • Mous Clarity 2.0 Case for Galaxy S23
    MagSafe case
    $65 at Amazon
  • Spigen Liquid Air for Galaxy S23
    Slim grip case
    $17 at Amazon
  • Bellroy Leather Case for Galaxy S23
    Stylish pick
    $45 at Amazon
  • Torro Leather Folio for Galaxy S23
    Leather folio case
    $38 at Amazon
  • Thinborne Aramid Fiber Case for Galaxy S23
    Thin case
    $40 at Amazon
